skipped_assessed_apr_19: geo-group (delta -0.9 confirm Apr 19; Adelanto trial still pending)
israel (delta confirm Apr 19; Sumud flotilla context)
south-sudan (delta confirm Apr 19; at floor; UNMISS Apr 30 watch)

results.name: Walt Disney
results.key_finding: April 14 2026 AI-rationale layoffs of ~1,000 employees + F500 DEI 65% disclosure decline move Disney from Established to Functional band. Severance generosity (4-month + bonus + health) prevents deeper drop.

results.name: Amazon
results.key_finding: PDX9 worker death with 'keep working around body' order + first-ever NLRB bargaining order are material EMP/INT signals, but net composite confirms Critical band. INT dimension moved -5.9 vs published reflecting NLRB appeal stance; at-floor absorption.

results.name: Pfizer
results.key_finding: Pfizer led 2026 drug price increases (80 products) despite Trump MFN deal — I1/I4 values inconsistency moves EQU and INT down 12.5 each, composite from Established to Functional.

results.name: UnitedHealth Group
results.hold: Earnings call Apr 21 — DO NOT finalize direction until earnings review
results.key_finding: HOLD — Q1 earnings Apr 21 under returned CEO Hemsley is material watch event. Delta +0.4 below threshold; no proposal today. Flag for 48-hour reassessment post-earnings.

results.name: China
results.key_finding: UN experts Jan 2026 forced-labour finding and Xinjiang supply-chain concerns corroborate Critical band. AWR/ACT/SYS downgrades offset by ACC/INT floor-clamping corrections. Net +1.9 — confirm.

results.name: Saudi Arabia
results.key_finding: Delta +5.0 at exact threshold is a floor-clamping/methodology correction, NOT substantive improvement. 356 executions in 2025 (second consecutive record) is the defining 2026 event. Recommend methodology review (same family as Haiti/South Sudan Apr 19 finding).

results.name: State Street
results.key_finding: F500 rank #1 at published 92.5 is not defensible. 900 Q2 2025 job eliminations + 2026 projected cuts + ESG language rollback + persistent 'layoff mill' Glassdoor reputation support -32.3 delta. Published uniform 4.5 indicates reputational rather than evidence-based scoring.

results.name: Microsoft
results.key_finding: January 2026 planned 11-22K layoffs + RTO 'soft layoff' mandate + DEI-criteria removal move Microsoft from Exemplary to Established. RAI Transparency Report + accessibility leadership anchor strongest dimensions.

results.name: Nucor
results.key_finding: No-layoff culture is genuine I1 strength; EAF steel 1/3 global GHG intensity is genuine SYS leadership. Published 87.4 overstates relative to peers; 2023-24 contractor fatalities and F500 DEI decline drive -21.0 delta.

results.name: Abbott Laboratories
results.key_finding: 782 NEC cases in MDL 3026 + $495M + $70M Similac verdicts + Abbott persistent denial pattern + 2025 Scarborough whistleblower lawsuit move Abbott from Exemplary to Functional. SYS medical-innovation strength anchors 68.8.

results.name: Ecolab
results.key_finding: Self-initiated PFAS phase-out by end 2026 is strongest AB2 signal among today's baselines. 2030 Positive Impact framework supports SYS 81.3. Published 87.4 overstates by 18.6 points.

confirmations_list.note: PDX9 + NLRB events absorbed at Critical floor

holds_list.reason: Q1 2026 earnings Apr 21 — first under returned CEO Hemsley

proposals_requiring_human_review: state-street (two-band move from F500 rank #1 — requires explicit sign-off even with high confidence)
abbott-laboratories (two-band move from rank #4 — NEC denial pattern is substantive evidence)
saudi-arabia (delta +5.0 at exact threshold; floor-clamping methodology concern; recommend hold at published)
walt-disney (established->functional band change on AI-layoff signal — confidence medium)
pfizer (established->functional band change on pricing pattern — confidence medium)
microsoft (one-band move from rank #3 — AI lab methodology cross-reference flag)
nucor (one-band move from rank #9 — evidence strong but contractor-vs-direct-employee distinction may warrant reviewer input)
ecolab (one-band move from rank #6 — strongest of baselines; SYS 81.3 legitimately Exemplary)
methodology_notes: Five rotation-backfill entities (State Street, Microsoft, Nucor, Abbott, Ecolab) all downgraded from Exemplary band by 18-32 points. Published F500 top-10 may systematically overstate — published uniform 4.5 scores across dimensions suggests reputational rather than dimension-specific scoring. Recommend methodology review of F500 top-tier published scores.

Saudi Arabia +5.0 delta is NOT a substantive improvement signal (execution record is worsening); it reflects floor-clamping methodology artifact where published subdims at 1.0 (0.0 scaled) understate the minimum institutional baseline. Same family as Apr 19 Haiti/South Sudan methodology flag. Recommend explicit floor-anchoring rule in methodology v1.1.

F500 sector DEI 65% disclosure decline now confirmed across 7 of 11 assessments today (Walt Disney, Microsoft, State Street, Nucor, Abbott, Ecolab, Pfizer). Consider sector-wide EQU baseline adjustment in next index rebuild rather than case-by-case.

Microsoft first-time baseline reveals cross-index consistency gap — Microsoft is in Fortune 500 at rank #3 but not in ai-labs index despite substantive AI footprint. Consider adding or explicit exclusion note in next rebuild.
UnitedHealth Group HOLD is the first use of earnings-timing-based hold rule — recommend documenting this pattern for future earnings-driven reassessments.

Amazon reassessment (second in 2 days) demonstrates 14-day lookback works: PDX9 + NLRB are material new 2026 events but absorbed at Critical floor without proposal churn. No methodology change needed.
